sections.paragraph:
ἐγκρίνω (ἐν + κρίνω) 1 aor. ἐνέκρινα, inf. ἐγκρῖναι (Eur.+; Pla., X.; CIG II, 2715a, 11 ἐ. εἰς τοὺς ἐφήβους; IG 7, 29, 6; Sb 9559, 10 [III b.c.]; EpArist 228; Jos., Bell. 2, 138) to make a judgment about someth. and classify it in a specific group, to class τινά τινι someone w. someone (Synes., Ep. 105 p. 250c) 2 Cor 10:12 (in a play on words w. συγκρῖναι compare).—M-M. s.v. ἐνκρίνω. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

Synes Synes , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 274
